latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 15:00
RNS Number : 2575C
Hammerson PLC
28 April 2026
Hammerson plc (the 'Company')
Notification of Transactions of Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ('PDMRs')
28 April 2026
The award of nil-cost options over ordinary shares of five pence each in the Company ('Shares') granted to Himanshu Raja on 27 April 2021 under the Hammerson plc Restricted Share Scheme (the 'Award'), ceased to be subject to a holding period and became exercisable on 27 April 2026. On 27 April 2026, Himanshu exercised the Award and sold 58,428 Shares to cover his tax and national insurance liability in respect of the Award. Himanshu has retained the remaining 65,450 Shares which were subject to the Award.
This announcement is made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The notification of dealing form can be found below.

latest.title: EMTN Programme: Publication of Base Prospectus
latest.content:
24 Apr 2026 13:30
RNS Number : 8728B
Hammerson PLC
24 April 2026
24 April 2026
Hammerson plc
EMTN Programme: publication of Base Prospectus
Hammerson plc announces that certain legal and regulatory updates and amendments have been made to the base prospectus (the "
Prospectus
") relating to the £5,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme under which Hammerson plc is an issuer. The Prospectus has been approved by the Financial Conduct Authority and has been published.
A copy of the Prospectus has been submitted to the National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at and can be downloaded from:
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
and
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/debt-investors
For further information:
Hammerson Investor Contacts
Josh Warren, Director of Group Performance and Investor Relations
T: +44 (0) 20 7887 1053 E:
josh.warren@hammerson.com
MHP for Hammerson Media
Oliver Hughes, Ollie Hoare and Charles Hirst
T: +44 (0)20 3128 8100 E:
Hammerson@mhpgroup.com
DISCLAIMER - INTENDED ADDRESSEES
EU MiFID II professionals/ECPs only/No EEA PRIIPs KID
UK MiFIR professionals/ECPs only/No UK DISC disclosure document
This announcement does not constitute or form part of an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to subscribe for or otherwise acquire any securities.
In particular, neither this announcement nor the Prospectus shall constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy securities in the United States or in any other jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration, exemption from registration or qualification under the securities law of any such jurisdiction.
The securities referred to herein have not been and will not be registered under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "
Securities Act
"), or with any securities regulatory authority of any State or other jurisdiction of the United States, and are subject to United States tax law requirements. Subject to certain exceptions, the securities referred to herein may not be offered, sold or delivered in the United States or to, or for the account or benefit of, U.S. persons (as defined in Regulation S under the Securities Act). The Prospectus may not be accessed from, or transmitted in or into, the United States. Accordingly, these securities may not be offered, sold or delivered in the United States.
Please note that the information contained in the Prospectus may be addressed to and/or targeted at persons who are residents of particular countries (specified in the Prospectus) only and is not intended for use and should not be relied upon by any person outside these countries and/or to whom the offer contained in the Prospectus is not addressed. Prior to relying on the information contained in the Prospectus, you must ascertain from the Prospectus whether or not you are part of the intended addressees of the information contained therein.
Your right to access this service is conditional upon complying with the above requirements.
The announcement above has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. .

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
8 Apr 2026 16:00
RNS Number : 6832Z
Hammerson PLC
08 April 2026
Hammerson plc (the 'Company')
Notification of Transactions of Directors and PDMRs
8 April 2026
The Company has been notified by Global Shares Trustees (UK) Limited, the trustee of the Hammerson Share Incentive Plan (the 'SIP'), (the 'SIP Trustee'), that on 2 April 2026, it awarded Partnership Shares (as defined in the rules of the SIP) of ordinary 5 pence shares in the Company ('Ordinary Shares') as follows: (i) 613 Shares on behalf of Grégoire Peureux, a Person Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ('PDMR'); and (ii) 613 Shares on behalf of Alex Dunn, a PDMR of the Company. The Ordinary Shares were purchased at a price of £2.93800 per Ordinary Share.
Under the SIP, the SIP Trustee will award each participating employee one Matching Share (as defined in the rules of the SIP) for each Partnership Share purchased by the employee. On 2 April 2026, the SIP Trustee therefore made awards at nil-cost to satisfy the Matching Shares element of the SIP as follows: (i) Grégoire Peureux 613 Ordinary Shares; and (ii) Alex Dunn 613 Ordinary Shares.
This announcement is made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The notification of dealing forms can be found below.

company_30s.what_they_do: Hammerson plc owns, manages and develops high‑quality shopping centres across the UK. They lease retail space to a mix of well‑known national and international brands and generate income mainly through long‑term lease agreements and property management fees.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK retail property market is a pillar of the economy, providing essential shopping infrastructure for millions of households. Hammerson’s portfolio includes several high‑traffic locations in major cities, giving it a defensible asset base that benefits from steady footfall and a diversified tenant mix.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently announced a new CEO, a dividend increase, and a fresh EMTN programme aimed at refinancing. These moves suggest a shift towards stabilising cash flow and signalling confidence to investors after a period of volatility.

rns_narrative: The company is in the middle of a corporate reset: a new chief executive is taking the helm, a dividend has been raised to 8.07p per share, and a large EMTN issuance is being launched to refinance debt and fund buy‑backs.
Governance changes – a new director and updated voting‑rights structure – indicate a tightening of control and a clearer strategic direction.
The annual report shows a solid results package and a plan for continued property acquisitions, signalling that the firm is positioning itself for a longer‑term rebound.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a dividend bump and fresh capital raising.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some investors see the dividend hike and the new CEO as a sign that the business is ready to grow again. They expect the EMTN to clean up the balance sheet and enable future buy‑backs.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the dividend payout will deplete cash reserves, that retail footfall could stay weak, and that the debt refinancing may not be enough to offset the heavy cost of maintaining a large property portfolio.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from pure uncertainty to a cautious hope that the company’s recent actions could unlock value. The tone is still subdued, but the few bullish voices hint at a potential turnaround.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no public interest spikes. This silence is a green light for a contrarian play because surprise is likely if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The dividend declaration on 25 Feb 2026 and the announcement of a higher payout per share could lift sentiment and attract income‑focused traders.
The EMTN programme’s launch on 24 Apr 2026 may signal that the company is tightening its balance sheet; a successful subscription could reduce debt and boost confidence.
The new CEO’s first quarterly report (mid‑Q3) will test whether the strategic shift is tangible – a strong earnings beat or a clear growth plan could trigger a bounce.
Any announcement of a new property acquisition or a lease renewal at a high‑traffic site would reinforce the narrative that the portfolio is still attractive.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signs to confirm the thesis: a sudden surge in daily volume around the 4.09 support level, a sharp uptick in the share price on the EMTN launch day, or a positive earnings surprise that lifts the dividend further.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash bottom. The APEX score indicates tight compression, suggesting that the market may be waiting for a decisive catalyst. A mean‑reversion is expected once the new CEO’s strategy starts to materialise and the debt profile improves.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Hammerson has just pulled a dividend hike and a fresh debt‑refinancing package – classic signs of a company that believes it can generate enough cash to reward shareholders. If the share price climbs back to the 4.09 level that the market has been circling, the upside is substantial, potentially several times the current price. The historical record for deep‑crash recoveries shows that a 4‑to‑5x return is realistic when the fundamentals improve.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the dividend payout could leave the company with insufficient liquidity to weather a prolonged retail downturn. If footfall remains weak and the EMTN does not raise enough capital, the share could stay trapped at very low levels. The worst‑case scenario is a further slide into a deeper trough, with the company needing to consider additional debt or asset sales.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low price – a small amount of capital is at stake. Potential gain could be 4‑5 times that amount if the price reaches the 4.09 support, giving an asymmetry of roughly 4:1 or higher.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 4.09 support level, ideally after a volume spike that confirms conviction. Use a position size that keeps risk below 5 % of the portfolio. Place a stop just below the support to protect against a false break.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the EMTN launch or the first quarterly earnings report. Watch for a clear earnings beat or a new lease signing announcement. A sharp uptick in volume on those dates would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified; therefore, a bullish stance remains viable. If a delisting notice or bankruptcy filing were to surface, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Hammerson sits at a deep crash bottom, its new dividend and debt‑refinancing offer a clear catalyst window, and the risk/reward asymmetry favours a contrarian bullish stance.
